key_insight: Geometry-only leverage scores can choose a much smaller, label-free training subset that still reaches near-plateau accuracy for linear ACE potentials.
engineering_relevance: This can cut DFT labeling cost by about 23x for ASSYST-generated training pools while preserving force, energy, and defect-level performance.
method: Compute statistical leverage scores from structure descriptors in ASSYST pools
Use CUR-type, leverage-guided subset sampling for iterative training
Compare against random, energy-based, and force-based selection baselines
evidence_signal: In Al benchmarks, leverage-selected subsets achieved plateau-level energy and force accuracy using roughly 30% to 40% of the labeled data, with alloy transfer tests showing comparable defect energetics once chemical diversity was sufficient.
watch_item: The gain depends on having a well-structured pool and enough chemical/defect diversity, so performance may drop if the candidate set is not representative.
paper_title: Data-Efficient Training of Linear ACE Potentials through Leverage-Guided Subset Selection of ASSYST Structure Pools
